brief_justification: Arrives at the exact correct answer (Yes/MST) with valid component-by-component mapping; goes one level beyond surface by decomposing each problem into graph primitives and noting why minimality implies a tree, but does not trace second-order implications; no explicit naming of biases, assumptions checked, or course-corrections — just linear assertion; no counterfactual checks, boundary conditions, or re-derivation from a different angle are performed; conclusions presented as certain with no acknowledgment that 'no road is redundant' could be interpreted differently or that the problems might admit alternate formulations; does not consider whether B or C could map to Steiner tree, connected subgraph minimization, or other network-design variants; most derivation steps are shown (vertex/edge/weight mappings stated), though intermediate reasoning about why minimality forces n-1 edges is asserted rather than fully derived.
